node.title: Uber | SDE II | Bangalore | Oct-Nov 2020 | OFFER

topic.title: Uber | SDE II | Bangalore | Oct-Nov 2020 | OFFER

post.content:
Status: 4+ years of experience, mostly in e-commerce core teams
Position: SDE II at a payments org in Bangalore
Location: Bangalore India
Date: October last week and Novemeber 1st week, 2020

Hackerrank screening round - 
1. Sliding window related question. I had already solved it in past on some platform I don't remember.
2. Very simple iteration problem
Don't remember both problems but second problem was very simple, brute force worked for about 90% of test cases.

Algo/DS problem solving round - 
1. Previous experience discussion
2. Possible to reach point A to B, if there are some walls in the matrix? Walls are sides of a cell

Machine coding round - 
1. Implement an in-memory queue like kafka and multiple producers/consumers on it.

Hiring Manager round - 
This was the most difficult I felt and this is where I fumbled.
1. Previous experience with all the design choices we as a team made and would I still make those choices if conditions would vary. Gave multiple scenarios and had discussions on them in depth. Questions on performance of tools we were using and how we can guarantee expected performance from them. This round lasted little more than 1.5 hours.

Bar Raiser round - 
Again discussion on previous experience and choices. Overall work culture and what changes I could make if given a chance. And other behavioural questions.

System Design Round - 
1. Design a chat system with online mark and blue ticks like whatsapp. For those who don't use whatsapp - blue ticks means the message was read by reviever.
2. Some questions on past experiences again.


The pattern is very much like what you would find here on leetcode experiences. Leetcode practice and friends who had applied few weeks back gave confidence.

Seems like your 3 rounds was on your previous projects
what all questions they asked if you could explain a bit?

have you worked on distributed project?

Congrats
What was expected in machine coding round?

its difficult to remember kakfa api's
did he asked to write everything or just interface level?
thanks
Hey Congratulations!\nIt will be helpful for us if you can share your machine coding round code. If you can publish it on anonymous website like pastebin. Many Thanks!
Congrats!! Can you brief us on how you prepared or approached machine coding round?
Congrats for cracking it\nBy e-commerce , do you mean you are working in amazon or flipkart?
